sprint: Vihu Card Platform v1 -- Redeem moved to Screen 1 + distinct marking + wireframe-matched board
task: Two real product findings from a real World Library screenshot, both fixed: (1) the Redeem widget lived on Screen 2, whose World Library row is filtered to one Creation Type -- redeeming a Card for a Theme outside the currently-active type registered correctly but then vanished from view, reading as 'nothing happened'. Fixed by moving Redeem to Screen 1 (before any Creation Type is chosen -- no category to get wrong) and replacing the old refreshWorldScreen() repaint with a real router, _routeAfterRedeem(), that reads the freshly-registered theme's own supportedCreationTypes and auto-navigates on a single match, or stays put and names the real matching types on 0/many. (2) Card-unlocked Worlds had no visual distinction from ordinary published ones -- ThemeRegistry already stamped redeemed/redeemedExpiresAt on the record, creationFlow.js just hadn't read it; added a small card-glyph corner badge + time-left caption on the World Library card and a matching 'Unlocked with a Card' note in the Selected World Preview. Same-day follow-up: the redeem board's CSS was restyled from a plain light-paper look to match the validated wireframe artifact's dark VihuCard starfield aesthetic (radial-gradient navy/purple board, gold border and glow, monospace grid labels/status text) -- deliberately reusing the exact hex values already established in the Card Art canvas's _drawCardBack(), so the printed card and the live redemption board read as the same object. Verified via an updated 54-assertion Playwright suite plus the existing 12-assertion Card Art suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged -- no schema/RPC/js/cardPlatform.js change. See CLAUDE.md's Vihu Card Platform v1 entry for the full account.
